title: End-to-End Synthetic Pipeline: CoI → SPONGE → Oblique FIGS with Module-Accuracy Correlation
summary: Demo notebook for the end-to-end synthetic pipeline comparing 5 FIGS tree methods (axis-aligned, random-oblique, signed spectral via SPONGE, unsigned spectral, hard-threshold). Generates a 2-module XOR dataset (5000 samples, 10 features), computes pairwise Co-Information matrices to discover feature interactions, clusters features using three methods (SPONGE signed spectral, unsigned spectral, hard threshold), then trains oblique FIGS models. Runs in ~8s with 3-fold CV, producing meaningful accuracy differences showing oblique methods outperform axis-aligned on XOR-structured data. Includes visualization comparing demo results with full-scale pre-computed results across all 6 dataset variants.
